summary: Verified the new premium /about-us page end-to-end against the spec. All 6 sections render in correct DOM order (about-hero → about-mission → about-pillars → about-enbref → about-why → about-cta). FR content, EN translations, gold-highlighted spans, 3 pillar cards with icon+h3+p, 7 en-bref service rows (exact French labels), 4 collage images all loaded (naturalWidth=600, no 404), 4 'Why Baked' benefit cards, animated counters resolve to '6+', '1', '100%', CTA gold words 'simplifié' & 'multipliées' with correct buttons → /#services and /#mobile. Header nav 'QUI SOMMES-NOUS' link has text-brand-gold ONLY when on /about-us; clicking it navigates to /about-us. Language switch flips all 6 section titles to EN, localStorage baked_locale='en', CTA EN string 'your everyday simplified, your opportunities multiplied' present. JSON-LD AboutPage script + meta description 'Découvrez la mission' present in HTML. Regression: homepage still renders 6 ecosystem cards in correct order, MobileShowcase still has exactly 4 phone dots.

ui_bugs.issue: Rendered <title> is 'À Propos de Nous | Baked Group — Baked Group' (the root layout template '%s — Baked Group' is being appended). The spec requires the exact string '<title>À Propos de Nous | Baked Group</title>'. Fix: in /app/frontend/src/app/about-us/page.tsx set `title: { absolute: 'À Propos de Nous | Baked Group' }` so the template is bypassed.

action_items: Use Metadata.title.absolute on /about-us page so the rendered <title> exactly matches 'À Propos de Nous | Baked Group' (current output has an extra ' — Baked Group' suffix from the layout-level template).
critical_code_review_comments: AboutUsContent.tsx is a single ~540-line client file with 5 sections + Counter + NetworkParticles. Consider splitting into one file per section under src/app/about-us/sections/* to improve maintainability and unlock per-section code-splitting.

JSON-LD <script> is injected as a sibling AFTER <Footer> inside <main>. This still works for SEO but the canonical place is inside the <head>. Move it via a child of next/script in app/about-us/page.tsx or put it inside a fragment rendered ahead of Header.

The hero title gold-span split logic uses heroTitle.split('NOUS') / .replace('US') which works today but would silently break for any future translation containing those substrings. Prefer storing parts as data (e.g. {pre, gold, post}) per locale.

Hero image, mission image and en-bref collage images are loaded from Unsplash hot-link URLs — if Unsplash rate-limits or blocks the production domain, the page degrades visually. Consider hosting locally under /public.

Header.tsx active-state logic uses hard-coded pathname checks; works but does not handle nested routes like /about-us/anything (would lose the active class). Use pathname.startsWith('/about-us') for resilience.
test_credentials: admin@baked.group / BakedAdmin2025! (regression item, not executed this iteration — was verified green in iteration_10)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Frontend at http://localhost:3000 is healthy. /about-us is fully wired and i18n-aware. Only outstanding deviation is the rendered <title> on /about-us — the root layout template appends ' — Baked Group'. To get an exact match to the spec, the page must set `title: { absolute: 'À Propos de Nous | Baked Group' }` (same EN counterpart if needed). Counters are framer-motion animated; if you re-test, scroll [data-testid="about-why"] into view and wait ~2s before reading their text. Useful testids: about-us-page, about-hero, about-hero-title, about-mission, about-pillars, pillar-0/1/2, about-enbref, about-why, about-cta, lang-button, lang-option-fr, lang-option-en.
rca of the issue: SEO title mismatch root cause: /app/frontend/src/app/layout.tsx defines `metadata.title.template = '%s — Baked Group'`. Next.js applies that template to every page's string-form title. /app/frontend/src/app/about-us/page.tsx sets `title: 'À Propos de Nous | Baked Group'` (a string), so Next.js renders '<title>À Propos de Nous | Baked Group — Baked Group</title>'. Mitigation: switch the about-us page metadata to `title: { absolute: 'À Propos de Nous | Baked Group' }` which suppresses the template.
